problems.diagnostic: /CaseProject/modified_model.aadl | line 36: named thing must be an expression with a type
problems.cause: AGREE requires expressions to refer to visible typed symbols; the port names are valid but the assume/guarantee clauses are tautological and misaligned with the signal flow intent.
problems.minimal_edit: Replace all assume/guarantee clauses with guarantee "signal_flow_x": acc_out_data_x = accelero_sub.acc_out_data_x; and guarantee "signal_flow_y": acc_out_data_y = accelero_sub.acc_out_data_y; — but these subcomponent references are not visible in component type scope, so remove all clauses and move to implementation annex.

executor_steps: Delete the entire annex agree block from ACCELERO (component type) because it contains invalid tautologies and cannot express the required signal flow using subcomponent ports (accelero_sub.*) which are not visible at the component type level.
Add a new annex agree block inside ACCELERO.IMPL (implementation) after the connections section.

In the implementation annex, use guarantee statements that reference the connection targets (acc_out_data_x, acc_out_data_y) and their source subcomponent ports (accelero_sub.acc_out_data_x, accelero_sub.acc_out_data_y) — these are now visible due to the implementation scope.

Write two guarantee clauses: guarantee "signal_flow_x": acc_out_data_x = accelero_sub.acc_out_data_x; and guarantee "signal_flow_y": acc_out_data_y = accelero_sub.acc_out_data_y; to formally encode the routing intent from R1 and R2.
